criteria.statement: An authenticated project member can create a conversation and list it in the same project.
Conversation and message history remains readable after the API process and browser session are restarted.
Replaying a message or run creation request with the same project-scoped idempotency key returns the original resource without duplicate model execution.
A run emits schema-valid, monotonically sequenced SSE events and resumes after Last-Event-ID without replay gaps or reordering.
Cancelling a queued or running run is idempotent, stops further model output, and persists the cancelled terminal status.
Retry requires a non-empty reason, creates a traceable new attempt, and never exceeds the configured maximum attempt count.
Two independently authenticated principals cannot read, stream, cancel, retry, or infer resources belonging to another project.
Every conversation, message, run, and event operation requires an authenticated project member role and is audited with actor and project identity.
Web liveness and API readiness endpoints report healthy only when their required runtime dependencies are available.
Database migrations complete successfully before application traffic is enabled and are safe to re-run.
Model execution uses the declared AI gateway policy and obtains credentials only from runtime secret injection; provider secrets never reach the browser or workspace.
Per-project and per-actor rate limits reject excess requests with a machine-readable error and Retry-After guidance.
A run that exceeds its configured timeout becomes failed exactly once, records a stable error code, and emits a terminal run.failed event.
Message and run retention jobs use the declared windows and redact secret-bearing diagnostic fields before persistence or export.
After an API or stream connection failure, the client reconnects from the last durable cursor and the run reaches one durable terminal state.
